scope.tagging_mechanism: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging with introduced_in_diff per AC-2.6 default-safe rule
scope.default_safe_contract: Findings with introduced_in_diff:true are NEW-in-this-diff and may force CLOSE: NO at the close.md verdict layer. Findings with introduced_in_diff:false are pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking. Untagged/absent defaults to ignore.

naming_violations.reason: TestReadGrant class-level docstring still says 'exact_or_substr semantics' but the class's read_grant() target now uses exact_only literal_policy (cycle 20260519-211515 Item D). The diff added two new test methods inside this class (test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_substring, test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_write_against_todowrite) that explicitly test exact_only behavior, making the class docstring contradict the tests it contains. The diff did not touch line 113 itself, but the diff's additions are the proximate cause of the inconsistency.
naming_violations.recommendation: Update class docstring to: '"""Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only read-only semantics)."""'
naming_violations.rationale_non_blocking: Documentation accuracy issue inside a test file's class docstring; does not affect functional correctness, hook behavior, or test discovery. Cosmetic minor — not a CLOSE: NO blocker per Section 5.4 cleanliness scope.

dead_functions.reason: Pre-cycle (git HEAD) _resolve_context() was called by _main() at line 372. The cycle's restructure of _main() (Item H) replaced that call with a direct _resolve_base_context() call and inlined the strict contract gate (Path A block at L495-L505). After the restructure, _resolve_context() has no caller in the file or anywhere else in the codebase — its only remaining reference is a backref comment inside _resolve_base_context's docstring (L417). This is a top-level private helper defined-but-never-called → dead code introduced by THIS diff.
dead_functions.verification: git show HEAD:hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py confirms _resolve_context was called at L372 pre-cycle; current file's grep for '_resolve_context' returns only the definition at L440 and one docstring backref at L417 — no production call sites.
dead_functions.recommendation: Delete _resolve_context() entirely (L440-L457). The 'strict contract gate' semantic it encapsulated is now inlined in _main() Path A (L495-L505) and is therefore not lost. Update the docstring backref at L417 of _resolve_base_context to remove the now-stale '_resolve_context gate-and-bail' reference, or replace with 'pre-cycle gate-and-bail'.
dead_functions.rationale_blocking: Per inspector rule #16 (Dead Functions Detection), top-level functions defined but never called are major-severity dead code. This was introduced by the cycle's diff (file-level: hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py). Although confined to a hook file and non-executing (no behavioral impact), it represents code-quality regression in the very file the cycle's main fix lands in — leaving it in place would normalize dead-code accretion in production-critical hook infrastructure.

verdict_input_for_close.explanation: 1 file-level finding tagged introduced_in_diff:true with blocks_close:true (dead_functions._resolve_context). 1 finding tagged introduced_in_diff:true but blocks_close:false (minor naming_violations stale class docstring — cosmetic). Per AC-2.6 default-safe rule, only the dead_functions finding may force CLOSE: NO at the close.md verdict layer; the close.md cleanliness gate evaluator should consider whether a single major dead-helper warrants CLOSE: NO or a fix-forward annotation, given the helper is non-executing and confined to a hook file.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 findings: (1) _resolve_context dead, (2) TestReadGrant docstring stale, (3) Path B header missing typed-lookup wording, (4) exact_or_substr branch in allowlist.py production-orphan, (5) OBSERVATION_ONLY clean naming. Findings 1 and 2 were verified and applied. Finding 3 was rejected after direct file read showed the Path B comment (L507-L510) already contains the typed-lookup precedence and no-fallthrough mandate codex suggested adding. Finding 4 was rejected as library-API design preservation: exact_or_substr is a generic policy option in _match_loaded_grant exercised by tests; the branch is not orphan code but a preserved API surface. Finding 5 was logged as observation_only.

findings.issue: _resolve_context() in hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py is now unreferenced after _main() switched to _resolve_base_context() and duplicated strict contract gating. Newly dead private helper code.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove _resolve_context() entirely; keep the 'strict contract gate' explanation in _main() if needed.
findings.rationale: Verified via git show HEAD vs current file grep: function had one caller pre-cycle (line 372 of _main()), now zero callers. Cycle's diff is the proximate cause. Flagged in dead_functions with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:true.

findings.issue: TestReadGrant class docstring still says 'exact_or_substr semantics' but read_grant() now uses exact_only.
findings.proposed_fix: Change to: '"""Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only read-only semantics)."""'
findings.rationale: Verified via grep: line 113 docstring contradicts the cycle's two new tests added inside the class. Although the diff did not literally touch line 113, the inconsistency is diff-introduced. Flagged in naming_violations with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:false (cosmetic). Codex's proposed wording adopted verbatim.

findings.issue: Path B header in hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py omits typed-lookup precedence / no-fallthrough wording.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace Path B paragraph with wording stating typed-lookup precedence and no-fallthrough on miss.
findings.rationale: Direct file read of lines 507-510 shows the Path B comment ALREADY contains the wording codex proposed: 'Case A (typed lookup via subagent_type) takes precedence over Case B (legacy in_progress inference). The two are mutually exclusive; Case A MUST NOT fall through to Case B on a typed miss (AC-H3b mandate).' Codex's read of the file was incomplete; the diff already addresses this concern.

findings.issue: exact_or_substr branch in allowlist.py is no longer production-called after read_grant() switched to exact_only; only tests call that helper policy.
findings.proposed_fix: Refactor bash branch 1 to call _match_loaded_grant with exact_or_substr, or delete the helper policy and tests.
findings.rationale: exact_or_substr is a generic literal_policy option in _match_loaded_grant — a library-API surface, not caller-specific dead code. It IS exercised by tests (test_exact_or_substr_exact_match, test_exact_or_substr_substring_match in TestMatchLoadedGrant). Deletion would be a breaking API change to a freshly-stabilized hook lib. The branch remains a valid future-caller option. This is library-design preservation, not orphan code. Out of scope for cleanliness inspector.

findings.issue: New helper/test naming and reference status look clean.
findings.rationale: _resolve_base_context, _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step, _extract_subagent_type are all called by _main() in the same file. New test method names are valid snake_case and unittest-discoverable. No new TODO/FIXME/legacy markers introduced by the diff. Confirmed via grep.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex Finding 1 (_resolve_context dead) applied as dead_functions finding with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:true. Codex Finding 2 (TestReadGrant stale docstring) applied as naming_violations finding with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:false (cosmetic). Codex Finding 3 rejected with file-read evidence. Codex Finding 4 rejected as library-API preservation. Codex Finding 5 logged as observation_only.
